analytics._note: PostHog, baked into the template so every customer site ships with analytics. One shared AlignCompass project; the slug is registered as a super-property so events filter per customer. Cookieless (memory persistence) so no consent banner is required. The apiKey is the PUBLIC project key — safe to embed. The build leaves this as-is.

seo.title: Put What You Can't Get To on Autopilot | AlignCompass
seo.description: You're great with customers — you just can't answer with your hands full. AlignCompass texts back missed calls, re-books past customers, and gets you reviews. Pay when it works.
seo.h1: Do the work you love. We'll catch everything you can't get to.
keywords: missed call text back for contractors
done for you marketing for trades

sections.eyebrow: For owners who love the work — and the customers
sections.headline: Do the work you love. We'll catch everything you can't get to.
sections.subhead: You love your customers and you're proud of your work — you just can't ask for a review, answer every call, or chase last year's customers while you're under a house. So I fill every one of those avenues for you: more 5-star reviews, missed calls texted back in seconds, past customers re-booked — landing in the system you already use, or one I set up for you. None of it replaces you. It just makes sure you get paid. Your website's free, and you only pay once it pays for itself.

image.alt: A 3D glass crystal compass slowly turning, its refracted light leaking soft rainbow edges on a dark field

sections.heading: You can't be two places at once. Right now that's costing you.
items: Miss a handful of calls a week and that's easily a thousand dollars walking to whoever picked up — about 85% of people who hit your voicemail never call back.
The customers you already wowed forget your name within a year and call a competitor — even though they were yours to keep.
Every review you didn't get around to asking for is a customer who picks the shop with more stars than you.

sections.heading: Everything you can't get to, handled.

items.title: Get more 5-star reviews — start here
items.body: Let's be real: reviews are what get you the next job, so this comes first. After every good job your customer gets a one-tap nudge to rate you where it matters — Google, Yelp, wherever you want the stars. And if someone's unhappy, I catch it privately, before it ever lands in public.

items.title: Answer every call — even the ones you miss
items.body: The second you can't pick up, the caller gets a real text back: "Sorry we missed you — what do you need?" Not a robot answering the phone. Nobody replies to those; people reply to a text. The job stays yours instead of going to the competitor — and you manage it in the system you already use, or one I set up and integrate for you.

items.title: Wake up the customers you already earned
items.body: They're sitting in your spreadsheet or your Jobber. You should call them — but who has the time? I automate the whole follow-up: maintenance reminders and "time for your next service," by text and email, so the ones who are ready raise their hand and you only spend time on the customers who actually want to book.

items.title: No customer list yet? We get you found.
items.body: Just starting out? We still might be a fit. I build your website, add a blog and optimize the posts, dial in your Google Business Profile, and start pulling reviews from anyone you've worked with — so you show up first when a neighbor searches for what you do.

items.title: Need something custom? I build it.
items.body: Booking platforms, web apps, even mobile apps — so customers can book you faster (not to replace you). We've built dispatch apps where customers pick passengers and bags and the next driver gets pinged to accept the ride. Selling a product? I build e-commerce stores too, so customers who'd rather not call can just buy. If it fills an avenue that gets you paid, I'll build it.

sections.heading: I'm picky about who I work with — in a good way.
sections.empathy: I'm not for the owner who'd rather never talk to a customer and wants a machine to run the whole thing. I'm for you: the one who actually likes your customers and takes pride in the job — you just can't pick up with your hand in a furnace or your name on a contract. Your cousin isn't answering the phone, and a front desk is a payroll you don't want yet. So what do you do? You put your business on autopilot. You handle everything you can — and the moment you can't get to something, that's where I come in. None of it replaces you. It just makes sure you get paid.
authority: Pay-when-it-works — you don't pay me until it covers itself (about 60 days)
Everything shows up as real booked jobs — in the system you already use, or one I set up for you
I'm a working business analyst, not a template reseller
I do 100% of the setup, and your customers and data stay yours

image.alt: A 3D glass crystal compass floating in a dark field, its needle catching soft refracted rainbow light

sections.heading: Getting started is the easy part.

steps.title: We build your free site and switch on call recovery
steps.body: A fast website that gets you found on Google — free — and an instant text back on every call you can't grab.

steps.title: We bring your past customers back to life
steps.body: We pull your list for you — most owners can't export it themselves — then warm them up so the ready ones come back first.

steps.title: The work lands where you already work
steps.body: Recovered calls, re-booked customers, new reviews, repeat jobs — all in the tools you already use. Point at a job and say, "that one came from the system."

sections.heading: Why the work you're already earning is leaking — and how we plug it

items.title: The demand is already yours. You're just losing it to a gap in your day.
items.body: Most local service businesses miss somewhere between a fifth and a third of their inbound calls — and solo operators miss far more, because you're on the job, not by the phone. Of the people who reach voicemail, about 85% never call back. That's not a problem you fix by buying more leads; it's a capture problem. On top of that, roughly 43% of a typical customer list has gone quiet — 18+ months with no booking — even though re-booking a past customer converts at 60–70%, versus 5–20% for a cold lead. And since the Google Map Pack drives over half of local-service search traffic, being easy to find and instant to answer is where booked jobs actually come from. AlignCompass plugs every leak: missed-call text-back so no caller slips away, reactivation so your own list goes back to work, and review and repeat-work automations that keep the flywheel turning — all measured in real booked jobs, not impressions.

sections.heading: The two questions every owner asks

items.q: "I already stay busy enough — why do I need this?"
items.a: Because busy and full aren't the same. You're still missing calls while you work, still letting past customers go cold, still leaving reviews unasked. This isn't more leads — it's keeping the business you already earn, and you see it as booked jobs, not a vague promise.
Only if it makes you money. The website's free, and you don't pay for the engine until it books enough to cover itself — about 60 days. If it doesn't pay for itself, you don't pay.

sections.heading: Simple pricing. You don't pay until it works.
includes: Automated 5-star review asks — on Google, Yelp, wherever matters (bad-review interception included)
Free SEO website + blog + Google Business Profile — yours to keep
Jobs land in your own system, or one I set up and integrate for you
I do all the setup — including pulling your customer list for you
Founding clients pay near cost for the first few slots in exchange for a testimonial

sections.heading: Want to see what you're leaving on the table?
sections.body: Book a free 15-minute call, or get a free missed-revenue audit that shows — in your own numbers — what unanswered calls and a cold customer list are costing you.

sections.headline: Everything we do points at one thing: more booked jobs.

sections.heading: The three pillars: reviews, calls, customers

items.title: Get more reviews — the one that gets you jobs
items.body: Reviews are what win the next customer, so this is pillar one. After every good job your customer gets a one-tap nudge to rate you where it counts — Google, Yelp, wherever you want the stars. Unhappy customers get routed to me privately first, so it's fixed before it ever goes public.

items.title: Never miss a call — and manage the job your way
items.body: You're on a job, the phone rings, and the caller's gone before you surface. Every missed call gets a real text back in seconds — not a robot — so the job stays yours. Then manage it in the system you already use, or one I set up and integrate for you.

items.title: Reactivate the customers you already earned
items.body: The people you already served drift to whoever markets to them next. I automate the follow-up — maintenance reminders and "time for your next service" by text and email — so the ready ones raise their hand and old jobs turn into new ones, at far better odds than chasing cold leads.

sections.heading: No customers yet? I get you found.

items.title: A website that works
items.body: A fast, SEO-strong website — free, and yours to keep.

items.title: A blog that ranks
items.body: I add a blog and optimize the posts, so homeowners find you before they even pick up the phone.

items.title: Google Business Profile, dialed in
items.body: Optimized so you show up in the Map Pack — where over half of local-service searches actually click.

items.title: Your first reviews
items.body: I start pulling reviews from anyone you've already worked with, so you launch with real credibility.

sections.heading: Need something custom? I build it.

items.title: Booking platforms, web apps, even mobile apps
items.body: So customers can book your services faster — not to replace you. We've built dispatch apps where customers pick passengers and bags and the next available driver gets pinged to accept the ride, round-robin and automatic. If your business needs booking and dispatch, I'll build it.

items.title: E-commerce stores
items.body: Selling a product — even if you already have a storefront? I build you a store so the customers who'd rather not call can just buy, and you get your product in their hands without the phone tag.

items.title: Automations that win repeat work
items.body: The follow-up before an appointment, the reminder six months later, the quiet "thanks — ready for the next one?" I wire up the touches that bring work back, whether or not you remember.

how much do missed calls cost

sections.headline: See exactly what you're leaving on the table — for free.

sections.heading: How the audit works

steps.title: Tell us a few numbers
steps.body: Roughly how many calls you get a week, and about how big your past-customer list is. Two minutes.

steps.title: We run the math
steps.body: We turn it into your real missed-revenue figure — no fluff, no vanity metrics.

steps.title: You decide
steps.body: Keep the numbers and do nothing, or book a free call to plug the leaks. No pressure.

sections.heading: Get your free missed-revenue audit
sections.body: Send us a few numbers and we'll come back with your figure. No obligation, no sales pressure.

sections.heading: Quick questions

items.a: Yes. No cost, no obligation, no card. You get the numbers whether or not we ever work together.

items.q: Will I get hounded with sales calls?
items.a: No. We send you the figure. If you want to talk, you book a call — on your terms.

items.q: What if my numbers are rough guesses?
items.a: Perfect. Ballpark is enough — the math still shows you the scale of what's slipping away.

seo.title: AlignCompass FAQ | Pricing, Guarantee & How It Works
seo.description: Answers on pricing, the pay-when-it-works guarantee, what's included, and how AlignCompass recovers missed calls, re-books past customers, and automates reviews.
seo.h1: Questions owners ask before they say yes.

sections.headline: Questions owners ask before they say yes.
sections.subhead: Straight answers on pricing, the guarantee, and exactly how this works — no jargon.

items.q: I already stay busy. Why do I need this?
items.a: Busy and full aren't the same. While you're working you're still missing calls, still letting past customers go cold, still leaving reviews unasked. We capture all of it, and you see it as booked jobs in your own system — not a vague promise.

items.q: Is this just another recurring fee that creeps onto my card?
items.a: Only if it makes you money. The website's free, and you don't pay for the engine until it books enough to cover itself — about 60 days. If it never pays for itself, you don't pay. That's the whole deal.
$297/mo plus a one-time setup of about $500–800. Founding clients pay near cost for the first few slots in exchange for a testimonial.

items.q: Isn't this just an AI robot answering my phone?
items.a: No. Nobody wants a robot picking up — and nobody replies to one. When you miss a call, the caller gets a real text back in seconds, so the conversation actually starts. People answer texts.

We build the free site, switch on missed-call recovery, reactivate your list, and turn on reviews. If that doesn't book enough to pay for itself in roughly 60 days, you don't start paying until it does.

items.q: What do I actually have to do?
items.a: Almost nothing. We do 100% of the setup — including pulling your customer list for you, which most owners can't export themselves. Your only job is replying to a one-tap text after a good job.

items.q: How fast will I see results?
items.a: Often within the first week — the first recovered call can land quickly, and a reactivation blast to your existing list is fast cash once we import it. Text-back goes live as soon as carrier approval clears.

items.q: Can you build something custom for my business?
items.a: Often, yes. Beyond the core engine we've built booking apps, dispatch tools, automations and blogs. If it gets you more booked work and it's a fit, we'll scope it with you honestly.

We're a newer operation building our first founding-client results right now, so we lead with your own numbers — a free audit that shows what you're losing — rather than someone else's case study. The mechanisms are well-proven across the industry, and we back the whole thing with pay-when-it-works.

items.q: Who owns my website and my data?
items.a: You do. The site is yours to keep, and your customer data stays yours — always.

sections.heading: Still have a question?
sections.body: Book a free call, or just email us — we answer straight.

footer.blurb: AlignCompass fills every avenue that gets you paid — more reviews, missed calls captured, past customers re-booked, even your website or store — so US local owners keep doing the work they love while the rest runs on autopilot.

flags: PREVIEW RESTYLE — Vivid+Co dark editorial design system, refined to match vividand.co. Canvas darkened from Slate Veil (#495764) to Carbon near-black (#0e1216) full-bleed, Bone White text (#fffdf9), Gunmetal Blue accent (#6f879c); no cards/shadows/radius. Added subtle ambient atmosphere: low-opacity warm-amber + cool-slate radial glow leaks from section corners plus a soft vignette (.ac-atmos / .ac-atmos--hero), keeping the canvas near-black not grey. Typeface unified to Inter 400/700 only; display headlines clamp up to ~136px pressed on the canvas. Buttons are outlined ghost rectangles. ALL copy, page structure, sections, CTAs, forms, SEO/schema/analytics preserved. Do not merge — preview only.

COMPASS MOTIF — Translucent glass compass with glowing rainbow (chromatic-aberration) edges, rendered LARGE + CENTERED so it overlaps/sits behind the hero headline (not a hard right-side block). Heroes render a Cloudinary-hosted looping <video> (publicId aligncompass-site/compass-glass-loop, autoplay+muted+loop+playsinline, poster=compass-glass-still) screen-blended over the near-black canvas (pure-black render drops out, only refracted light + glow remain), radial-masked + a soft glow halo (.ac-compass-halo) so it melts into the canvas with no hard edges; a centered near-black scrim bloom keeps Bone White headline contrast AA. prefers-reduced-motion and mobile fall back to the still (aligncompass-site/compass-glass-still). Guide sections use the masked still with a very slow rotation + halo. Both glass assets generated via Higgsfield (pure-black bg, strong R/G/B rainbow edge-leaks + bloom) and served only from Cloudinary (never hotlinked). Replaces the prior compass-loop/compass-still renders. Masking via .ac-compass (mix-blend screen + radial mask) + .ac-compass--float / --spin, all disabled under reduced-motion.

Copy pass 3 (Pedro voice): first-person founder voice; reviews elevated to #1 pillar; job management = use yours (integrate) or one I set up; added 'no customers yet' visibility path (site/blog/GBP/reviews); broadened custom builds to booking/web/mobile apps + e-commerce; framed as 'fill every avenue so you get paid.'
Palette reskinned to Cyprus (#004741) + Sand (#F0EDE4) per Pedro's reference card; accent #00695a. Replaces the prior slate/teal. Fonts unchanged (Archivo/Inter).

Copy rewritten in Pedro's voice: broadened from missed-call-only to the 'autopilot for what you can't get to' frame (capture + reactivation + reviews + repeat work + custom builds); sharpened the 'who we work with' stance (owners who love customers, not those avoiding them).

Primary CTA now resolves to /contact#book (the real contact form) so buttons work site-wide. Booking-WIDGET embed still pending: drop the booking-platform scheduler URL in at the contact section + NAP 'Booking' item when ready.
NAP incomplete: no phone number yet. Email-only NAP. Do not invent — supply a phone before adding it.
No first-party proof: testimonials section intentionally omitted site-wide until real (founding-client results pending).
Forms post via mailto:aligncompass@gmail.com (endpoint empty). Add a real form endpoint (Formspree-style) for reliable delivery.
Base is '/' (apex root site). vercel.json customer rewrites preserved.
